system_prompt:
You are an expert toxicologist with extensive knowledge in chemical safety assessment, toxicokinetics, and toxicodynamics. Your expertise includes:
1. Interpreting chemical structures and properties 2. Analyzing toxicological data from various sources (e.g., in vitro, in vivo, and in silico studies) 3. Applying read-across and QSAR (Quantitative Structure-Activity Relationship) approaches 4. Understanding mechanisms of toxicity and adverse outcome pathways 5. Evaluating systemic availability based on ADME (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion) properties 6. Assessing potential health hazards and risks associated with chemical exposure
When providing toxicological evaluations: - Use reliable scientific sources and databases (e.g., PubChem, ECHA, EPA, IARC) - Consider both experimental data and predictive models - Explain your reasoning and cite relevant studies or guidelines - Acknowledge uncertainties and data gaps - Provide a balanced assessment, considering both potential hazards and mitigating factors - Use a weight-of-evidence approach when multiple data sources are available - Classify toxicodynamic activity and systemic availability as high, medium, or low based on  the available evidence and expert judgment - When using read-across, clearly state the basis for the analogy and any limitations
Adhere to ethical standards in toxicology and maintain scientific objectivity in your assessments.

tests.prompt: Answer the following question with a list of string. List the {effect_type} of {chem_casrn} on rat

vars.chem_casrn: 6PPQ (N-(1,3-dimethylbutyl)-N'-phenyl-p-phenylenediamine) or its synonyms with CAS number 2754428-18-5

response.output:
[
"No in vivo respiratory-system toxicity data in rats have been located for 6PPQ (6PPD-quinone, CAS 2754428-18-5).",
"Consequently, respiratory irritation, sensitisation, or other pulmonary effects in rats remain uncharacterised (data gap)."
]

tests.prompt: Answer the following question with a list of string. List the {effect_type} of {chem_casrn} on rat

vars.chem_casrn: Phenanthrene or its synonyms with CAS number 85-01-8

response.output:
[
  "Pulmonary inflammation",
  "Respiratory tract irritation",
  "Alveolar macrophage accumulation",
  "Bronchiolar epithelial hyperplasia",
  "Nasal epithelial degeneration"
]
searched_keyphrases: Synonyms of Phenanthrene and rat lung toxicity
Pulmonary pathology due to Phenanthrene in rats
Rat respiratory system toxicity data for Phenanthrene

tests.prompt: Answer the following question with a list of string. List the {effect_type} of {chem_casrn} on rat

vars.chem_casrn: Lanthanum or its synonyms with CAS number 7439-91-0

response.output:
[
"Oral systemic bioavailability (F): ≈ 0.0007–0.002 (0.07 – 0.2 %) of the administered dose after single gavage of 14C-lanthanum carbonate (Pennick et al., 2006; Boulanger et al., 2005, rat).",
"Gastro-intestinal absorption: < 0.0005 fraction absorbed; > 99.5 % of oral dose recovered in faeces within 48 h (Addison et al., 2001, rat).",
"Plasma Cmax: ≈ 0.12 µg La/mL at 2 h post-dose after 0.5 mg/kg i.v. lanthanum chloride (Zhang et al., 2013, rat).",
"Volume of distribution at steady state (Vdss): ≈ 0.13 L/kg after i.v. lanthanum chloride 0.5 mg/kg bw (Zhang et al., 2013, rat).",
"Plasma protein binding: > 99.9 % for lanthanum ions in rat serum (ultrafiltration; Pennick et al., 2006).",
"Initial distribution half-life (α t½): ≈ 1.3 h; terminal elimination half-life (β t½): ≈ 53 h after i.v. lanthanum chloride (Zhang et al., 2013, rat).",
"Total systemic clearance (Cl): ≈ 1.5 mL/h/kg after i.v. lanthanum chloride 0.5 mg/kg (Zhang et al., 2013, rat).",
"Tissue distribution: highest accumulation in bone (up to ~ 70 µg/g after 13-week exposure); bone elimination half-life ≈ 42 days (Pennick et al., 2006, rat).",
"Excretion route: > 99 % of administered La eliminated via faeces; urinary excretion < 0.2 % of dose (Boulanger et al., 2005, rat).",
"Blood–brain barrier penetration: extremely low; brain concentrations < 0.01 µg/g even at high doses (Addison et al., 2001, rat)."
]
